description: async void should normally be limited to event handlers because callers cannot await or catch it.
body: Use when async errors are unobserved. Do not use async void in services, controllers, or tests unless framework signature requires it. Return Task and await. Verify negative async tests. Risk boundary: process-level exception handling and flaky tests. Use when C# / .NET Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
